pr.title: Split out some sections based on surrounding splits
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large mechanical assembly-file reorganization that split anonymous asm/melee/text_N.s sections into more specific subsystem paths based on nearby/surrounding splits. The PR added new asm slices such as asm/melee/mp/code_80041C78.s and asm/melee/pl/code_8003715C.s, renamed asm/melee/text_5.s to asm/melee/gm/code_801601C4.s with 100% similarity, moved large ranges between text_2/text_3/text_4/text_6/text_7, and updated obj_files.mk to reflect the new object list. No review comments or PR body text were present in the slice.
what_changed: Created asm/melee/mp/code_80041C78.s as a new assembly split containing a .text range starting at 0x80041C78 and symbols such as func_80041C78, func_80041C8C, func_80041DD0, func_80041EE4, and func_80042078.
Created asm/melee/pl/code_8003715C.s as a new assembly split, replacing content previously embedded in a broader text_N.s file according to changed-file counts.
Renamed asm/melee/text_5.s to asm/melee/gm/code_801601C4.s with 100% similarity, indicating a pure path/name move rather than code edits.
Moved a very large amount of assembly out of asm/melee/text_7.s into asm/melee/text_4.s, with text_7.s showing 287764 deletions and text_4.s showing 287762 additions.
Moved another large block out of asm/melee/text_6.s into asm/melee/text_3.s and/or newly split subsystem files, with text_6.s deleted by 44918 lines and text_3.s gaining 44916 lines.
Reduced asm/melee/text_2.s while adding asm/melee/pl/code_8003715C.s, suggesting extraction of a player-related code range from the generic text bucket.
Updated obj_files.mk with small object-list edits so the build used the new split assembly file paths instead of only the old generic text_N.s files.
smart_moves: Used subsystem directories and start-address-based filenames, e.g. mp/code_80041C78.s, pl/code_8003715C.s, and gm/code_801601C4.s, to make raw asm ranges easier to locate and later replace with C.
Preserved exact assembly content during at least one move, shown by the 100% similarity rename from text_5.s to gm/code_801601C4.s.
Kept generic text_N.s files as transitional buckets while carving out ranges with clearer ownership, minimizing semantic risk during a repository-wide re-split.
Updated obj_files.mk in the same PR as the assembly moves, keeping source layout and build ordering synchronized.
decomp_lessons: When a raw asm region can be associated with a nearby subsystem split, move it into that subsystem directory even before functions are decompiled; this improves searchability and ownership for future work.
For un-decompiled assembly, filenames of the form code_<start address>.s are useful stable anchors until meaningful source names are known.
Large asm splits should be treated as mechanical moves unless there is evidence of instruction edits; preserving similarity and updating build lists are the key correctness checks.
Keep .include "macros.inc" and explicit .section .text address comments in new asm split files so the moved file remains buildable and address-auditable.
After moving assembly between text_N.s buckets and subsystem files, obj_files.mk must be updated immediately to preserve link order and object inclusion.
naming_conventions: Subsystem asm paths use asm/melee/<subsystem>/code_<hex start address>.s when the code does not yet have a higher-level source filename.
Generic unsorted assembly buckets use asm/melee/text_N.s.
Assembly labels and functions in the moved code retain address-derived names such as func_80041C78 and lbl_80041D9C.
Small data references retain address-derived SDA labels such as lbl_804D64A0, lbl_804D7F90, and _SDA_BASE_/_SDA2_BASE_ relocations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mechanical asm movement by contiguous address range rather than rewriting functions.
Use of Git rename detection for pure moves, evidenced by similarity index 100% for text_5.s to gm/code_801601C4.s.
Preserved raw PowerPC assembly formatting with address comments, global labels, branch labels, and macros.inc includes.
Maintained build integration by editing obj_files.mk alongside the file splits.
searchable_terms: Split out some sections based on surrounding splits
follow_up_queries: Check obj_files.mk around PR 74 to confirm the exact object ordering changes for the new mp/pl/gm split files.
Search later PRs for asm/melee/mp/code_80041C78.s to see when this raw assembly range was converted to C or renamed.
Search later PRs for asm/melee/pl/code_8003715C.s and asm/melee/gm/code_801601C4.s to track subsequent decompilation ownership.
Compare the pre- and post-PR map/link order if investigating whether this split affected binary matching.
